title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 22.
content:
There was a Young person of Crete,
    Whose toilette was far from complete;
    She dressed in a sack,
    Spickle-speckled with black,
    That ombliferous person of Crete.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 22.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
